verses.sanskrit: आर्तस्वरम् तु तम् भर्तुः विज्ञाय सदृशम् वने ।उवाच लक्ष्मणम् सीता गच्छ जानीहि राघवम् ॥३-४५-१॥
verses.text:
On identifying the grievous voice that is identifiable with her husband's voice, Seetha spoke to Lakshmana, 
"you go at once, and check up on Raghava.' [3-45-1]

verses.sanskrit: न हि मे जीवितम् स्थाने हृदयम् वा अवतिष्ठते ।क्रोशतः परम आर्तस्य श्रुतः शब्दो मया भृशम् ॥३-४५-२॥
verses.text:
"I have heard the loud yelling voice of highly fretful Rama whereby my heart, or my very entity, is 
not abiding in its place, and it will be apt of you to protect such a brother of yours who is screaming 
in the forest. [3-45-2, 3a]

verses.sanskrit: आक्रन्दमानम् तु वने भ्रातरम् त्रातुम् अर्हसि ।तम् क्षिप्रम् अभिधाव त्वम् भ्रातरम् शरण एषिणम् ॥३-४५-३॥
verses.text:
"He might have come under the subjugation of demons as with a bull coming under the seize of lions, 
hence, you hurry up so as to near such a brother of yours who is seeking protection." So said Seetha 
toi Lakshmana. [3-45-3b, 4a]

verses.sanskrit: रक्षसाम् वशम् आपन्नम् सिंहानाम् इव गोवृषम् ।न जगाम तथा उक्तः तु भ्रातुः आज्ञाय शासनम् ॥३-४५-४॥
verses.text:
Although she spoke a lot to him in this way, Lakshmana on his part has not moved out remembering his 
brother's order to stay guard to Seetha, and hence that Janaki, the daughter of Janaka, who is already 
agitated for Rama further spoke to him in the paradox of his not going out of hermitage. [3-45-4b, 5a]

verses.sanskrit: तम् उवाच ततः तत्र क्षुभिता जनक आत्मजा ।सौमित्रे मित्र रूपेण भ्रातुः त्वम् असि शत्रुवत् ॥३-४५-५॥
verses.text:
"You are like a foe of your brother in friend's mien, Soumitri, as you are not making a move towards 
a brother even if he is in an emergency. [3-45-5b, 6a]

verses.sanskrit: यः त्वम् अस्याम् अवस्थायाम् भ्रातरम् न अभिपद्यसे ।इच्छसि त्वम् विनश्यन्तम् रामम् लक्ष्मण मत् कृते ॥३-४५-६॥
verses.text:
"Because of me you wish Rama to be completely destroyed, and only because of your cupidity for me you 
are not following up on Raghava. It is definite. [3-45-6b, 7a]

verses.sanskrit: लोभात् तु मत् कृतम् नूनम् न अनुगच्छसि राघवम् ।व्यसनम् ते प्रियम् मन्ये स्नेहो भ्रातरि न अस्ति ते ॥३-४५-७॥
verses.text:
" 'Raghava in direst strait' is the most liked thing to you, thus I deem, as you have no heart for that 
brother, and as you complacently stay behind even when that great-resplendent Rama is unseen. [3-45-7b, 
8a]

verses.sanskrit: तेन तिष्ठसि विस्रब्धः तम् अपश्यन् महाद्युतिम् ।किम् हि संशयम् आपन्ने तस्मिन् इह मया भवेत् ॥३-४५-८॥
verses.text:
"After which helmsman you have come here that Rama is undergoing an uncertainty of life-and-death, as 
such I indeed wonder what business you have here now in staying with me at this hermitage?" Seetha spoke 
so to Lakshmana. [3-45-8, 9a]

verses.sanskrit: कर्तव्यम् इह तिष्ठन्त्या यत् प्रधानः त्वम् आगतः ।एवम् ब्रुवाणम् वैदेहीम् बाष्प शोक समन्वितम् ॥३-४५-९॥
verses.text:
Lakshmana spoke to Seetha, the princess from Videha kingdom, who is talking deluged under tears and 
anguish in that way, as she is fretful like a she-deer. [3-45-9a, 10b]

verses.sanskrit: अब्रवीत् लक्ष्मणः त्रस्ताम् सीताम् मृग वधूम् इव ।पन्नग असुर गन्धर्व देव दानव राक्षसैः ॥३-४५-१०॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Vaidehi, your husband is an impossible one for vincibility by serpents, asura-s, gandharva-s, gods, 
fiends, demons, no doubt about it.

verses.sanskrit: अशक्यः तव वैदेही भर्ता जेतुम् न संशयः ।देवि देव मनुष्येषु गन्धर्वेषु पतत्रिषु ॥३-४५-११॥
verses.text:
"Oh, auspicious one, unmarked is some combatant who can counterattack Rama from among gods, humans, 
gandharva-s, birds, ogres, kinnaraa-s, beasts, or oh, lady, even from horrendous demons, as Rama vies 
with Indra in any given war. [3-45-11b, 12, 13a]

verses.sanskrit: राक्षसेषु पिशाचेषु किन्नरेषु मृगेषु च ।दानवेषु च घोरेषु न स विद्येत शोभने ॥३-४५-१२॥
यो रामम् प्रतियुध्येत समरे वासव उपमम् ।अवध्यः समरे रामो न एवम् त्वम् वक्तुम् अर्हसि ॥३-४५-१३॥
verses.text:
"It is inapt of you to talk that way as Rama is inextirpable in a given war, and as for me, I do not 
venture to abandon you in this forest in the absence of Raghava. [3-45-13b, 14a]

verses.sanskrit: न त्वाम् अस्मिन् वने हातुम् उत्सहे राघवम् विना ।अनिवार्यम् बलम् तस्य बलैः बलवताम् अपि ॥३-४५-१४॥
verses.text:
"Let the most forceful Crowns with all their military forces, or, all of the gods together with their 
heads, why they, let all the three worlds put together come mutinously, whether jointly or severally, 
Rama's prowess is irrestrainable. [3-45, 14b, 15a]

verses.sanskrit: त्रिभिः लोकैः समुदितैः स ईश्वरैः स अमरैः अपि ।हृदयम् निर्वृतम् ते अस्तु संतापः त्यज्यताम् तव ॥३-४५-१५॥
verses.text:
"Hence, let your heart be diverted and anxiety divested, as your husband will be returning soon on killing 
that extraordinary deer. [3-45-15b, 16a]

verses.sanskrit: आगमिष्यति ते भर्ता शीघ्रम् हत्वा मृगोत्तमम् ।न सस् तस्य स्वरो व्यक्तम् न कश्चित् अपि दैवतः ॥३-४५-१६॥
verses.text:
"Evidently it is not the voice of Rama, nor that of any other god, but someone mimicked that voice for 
chicanery, and that must be the illusory voice of that demon Maareecha, similar in effect to the magical 
city of gandharva-s usually created by magicians in general. [3-45-15b, 16]

verses.sanskrit: गन्धर्व नगर प्रख्या माया तस्य च रक्षसः ।न्यास भूता असि वैदेहि न्यस्ता मयि महात्मना ॥३-४५-१७॥
verses.text:
"And you are an entrustment, oh, best lady Vaidehi, as the great-souled Rama has entrusted you in my 
security, as such I do not dare to leave you off now. [3-45-17, 18a]

verses.sanskrit: रामेण त्वम् वरारोहे न त्वाम् त्यक्तुम् इह उत्सहे ।कृत वैराः च कल्याणि वयम् एतैः निशाचरैः ॥३-४५-१८॥
verses.text:
"Oh, gracious lady, we have become the adversaries of these night-walkers insofar as the elimination 
of Khara and others, and concerning the eradication of Janasthaana. [3-45-18b, 19a]

verses.sanskrit: खरस्य निधने देवि जनस्थान वधम् प्रति ।राक्षसा विविधा वाचो व्यवहरन्ति महावने ॥३-४५-१९॥
verses.text:
"The demons are the swaggerers in violence in great forests and they will be employing various voices. 
Therefore Vaidehi, just do not bother about it." Thus Lakshmana advised Seetha. [3-45-18b, 19, 20a]

verses.sanskrit: हिंसा विहारा वैदेहि न चिन्तयितुम् अर्हसि ।लक्ष्मणेन एवम् उक्ता तु क्रुद्धा संरक्त लोचना ॥३-४५-२०॥
verses.text:
As for Seetha, she became furious on hearing Lakshmana's words of advice, and while her eyes are becoming 
bloodshot she spoke these rude sentences to that veracious Lakshmana. [3-45-20b, 21a]

verses.sanskrit: अब्रवीत् परुषम् वाक्यम् लक्ष्मणम् सत्य वादिनम् ।अनार्य करुणारंभ नृशंस कुल पांसन ॥३-४५-२१॥
verses.text:
"You are a debased one devoid of mercy, thus a dreadful one, and a degrader of your dynasty, and I deem 
that you derive pleasure from fatal hardship to Rama. [3-45-21b, 22a]

verses.sanskrit: अहम् तव प्रियम् मन्ये रामस्य व्यसनम् महत् ।रामस्य व्यसन्म् दृष्ट्वा तेन एतानि प्रभाषसे ॥३-४५-२२॥
verses.text:
"You are haranguing all these sermons only on noticing hardship to Rama. It is unsurprising that the 
pitiless ones and rivals of your kind, who always behave in a cloak-and dagger way, will tend to commit 
deadly sins, Lakshmana, and it is not uncommon. [3-45-22b, 23]

verses.sanskrit: न एव चित्रम् सपत्नेषु पापम् लक्ष्मण यत् भवेत् ।त्वत् विधेषु नृशंसेषु नित्यम् प्रच्छन्न चारिषु ॥३-४५-२३॥
सुदुष्टः त्वम् वने रामम् एकम् एको अनुगच्छसि ।मम हेतोः प्रतिच्छन्नः प्रयुक्तो भरतेन वा ॥३-४५-२४॥
verses.text:
"You are verily maleficent for you have singly and covertly followed Rama, who started to forests all 
alone, or instigated by Bharata, only because of me. [3-45-24]

verses.sanskrit: तत् न सिद्ध्यति सौमित्रे तव अपि भरतस्य वा ।कथम् इंदीवर श्यामम् रामम् पद्म निभेक्षणम् ॥३-४५-२५॥
verses.text:
"Whether this is an intrigue of yours or that of Bharata it is unachievable, Saumitri. How can I long 
for another commoner when I have put my faith only in the lotus-blue-complexioned and lotus-bright-eyed 
husband of mine, Rama? [3-45-25, 26a]

verses.sanskrit: उपसंश्रित्य भर्तारम् कामयेयम् पृथक् जनम् ।समक्षम् तव सौमित्रे प्राणान् त्यक्ष्यामि असंशयम् ॥३-४५-२६॥
verses.text:
"If that comes to that, I will just discard my life in your presence, Soumitri do not mistake it. I 
do not eke out an existence on this earth even for a moment without Rama." Thus Seetha threatened Lakshmana. 
[3-45-26b, 27a]

verses.sanskrit: रामम् विना क्षणम् अपि न एव जीवामि भू तले ।इति उक्तः परुषम् वाक्यम् सीतया रोमहर्षणम् ॥३-४५-२७॥
verses.text:
When that self-mortified Lakshmana is addressed in this way with hair-raising and acerbic words by Seetha, 
he spoke to her suppliantly adjoining his palm-fold. [3-45-27b, 28a]

verses.sanskrit: अब्रवीत् लक्ष्मणः सीताम् प्रांजलिः विजितेन्द्रियः ।उत्तरम् न उत्सहे वक्तुम् दैवतम् भवती मम ॥३-४५-२८॥
verses.text:
"I do not venture to reply you Maithili, as you are like a deity to me. Women using words that are in 
a class by themselves is not at all surprising. This sort of bringing stilted words into play is the 
nature of women and it is obvious in the world. [3-45-28b, 29]

verses.sanskrit: वाक्यम् अप्रतिरूपम् तु न चित्रम् स्त्रीषु मैथिलि ।स्वभावः तु एष नारीणाम् एषु लोकेषु दृश्यते ॥३-४५-२९॥
विमुक्त धर्माः चपलाः तीक्ष्णा भेदकराः स्त्रियः ।न सहे हि ईदृशम् वाक्यम् वैदेही जनक आत्मजे ॥३-४५-३०॥
verses.text:
"Women by their nature are unbound by the etiquette of decency, whimsical, cantankerous and they tend 
to become the artificers of vicissitudes, and oh, Vaidehi, the daughter of Janaka, indeed unbearable 
are this kind of words that are much the same as burnt iron arrows thrust in between my two ears. [3-45-30, 
31a]

verses.sanskrit: श्रोत्रयोः उभयोः मध्ये तप्त नाराच सन्निभम् ।उपशृण्वंतु मे सर्वे साक्षिनो हि वनेचराः ॥३-४५-३१॥
verses.text:
"Let all of the forest-itinerants indeed listen as to how you are addressing me with bitter words, though 
I am reasoning with you conscientiously, and let them become my corroborators for the sake of justice. 
[3-45-31b, 32a]

verses.sanskrit: न्याय वादी यथा वाक्यम् उक्तो अहम् परुषम् त्वया ।धिक् त्वाम् अद्य प्रणश्यन्तीम् यन् माम् एवम् विशंकसे ॥३-४५-३२॥
verses.text:
"Fie upon you, you are acrimoniously tempered owing to your faminality, and by which impulsive reason 
you mistrust me who am presently abiding in my brother's order, may God damn you for that crotchety. 
[3-45-32b, 33a]

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीत्वात् दुष्ट स्वभावेन गुरु वाक्ये व्यवस्थितम् ।गमिष्ये यत्र काकुत्स्थः स्वस्ति ते अस्तु वरानने ॥३-४५-३३॥
verses.text:
"I am going there where Rama is, oh, lady with best visage, you be blest, and oh, broad-eyed one, let 
all of the forest deities protect you. [3-45-33b, 34a]

verses.sanskrit: रक्षन्तु त्वाम् विशालाक्षि समग्रा वन देवताः ।निमित्तानि हि घोराणि यानि प्रादुर्भवन्ति मे ।अपि त्वाम् सह रामेण पश्येयम् पुनरागतः ॥३-४५-३४॥
verses.text:
"Gauging by those dangerous forebodings that are now bidding fair, can I to see you again along with 
Rama on my return, or not? That I do not know." Thus Lakshmana spoke to Seetha. [3-45-34b, c]

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्मणेन एवम् उक्ता तु रुदती जनकाअत्मजा ।प्रत्युवाच ततो वाक्यम् तीव्रम् बाष्प परिप्लुता ॥३-४५-३५॥
verses.text:
When Lakshmana spoke to that wailful daughter of Janaka, she on her part spoke this curt sentence to 
him, while tears whelmed her to a fault. [3-45-35]

verses.sanskrit: गोदावरीम् प्रवेक्ष्यामि हीना रामेण लक्ष्मण ।आबन्धिष्ये अथवा त्यक्ष्ये विषमे देहम् आत्मनः ॥३-४५-३६॥
verses.text:
"Without Rama I plunge myself in River Godavari, or else I string up myself, or discard this body of 
mine from heights..." Thus Seetha started to vent out her feelings. [3-45-36]

verses.sanskrit: पिबामि वा विषम् तीक्ष्णम् प्रवेक्ष्यामि हुताशनम् ।न तु अहम् राघवात् अन्यम् कदाअपि पुरुषम् स्पृशे ॥३-४५-३७॥
verses.text:
"I drink deadly poison, or I enter a flaring fire, but I will never touch another man other than Raghava. 
[3-45-37]

verses.sanskrit: इति लक्ष्मणम् आश्रुत्य सीता दुह्ख समन्विता ।पाणिभ्याम् रुदती दुह्खाद् उदरम् प्रजघान ह ॥३-४५-३८॥
verses.text:
Seetha on declaring to Lakshmana in this way, she who is anguished and weeping bitterly, then started 
to smite her belly with both of her palms in a heightened agony. [3-45-38]

verses.sanskrit: ताम् आर्त रूपाम् विमना रुदन्तीम्सौमित्रिः आलोक्य विशाल नेत्राम् ।आश्वासयामास न चैव भर्तुःतम् भ्रातरम् किंचित् उवाच सीता ॥३-४५-३९॥
verses.text:
Observing the agonised aspect of Seetha that cheerless Saumitri started to cheer her up by repeatedly 
informing that her husband Rama will come soon. Even then Seetha has not spoken in the least to the 
brother of her husband, and maintained a silence of antipathy. [3-45-39]

verses.sanskrit: ततः तु सीताम् अभिवाद्य लक्ष्मणःकृत अन्जलिः किम्चिद् अभिप्रणम्य ।अवेक्षमाणो बहुशः स मैथिलीम्जगाम रामस्य समीपम् आत्मवान् ॥३-४५-४०॥
verses.text:
But then, holding Seetha in usual reverence that self-respectful Lakshmana briefly came to her fore 
making a reverential palm-fold that briefly, and then he proceeded to the proximity of Rama, while repeatedly 
looking back at that lonely lady in the thick of forest. [3-45-40]
